description:
You are an MLOps engineer who needs to index legacy experiment artifacts so the research team can search them later. Inside /home/user/experiments you will find several *immediate* sub-directories (e.g. exp001, exp002, …). Every one of those sub-directories contains a file named config.ini that follows the standard INI syntax. From each config.ini file, pull the exact value of the following keys (all keys are located at top-level sections—no nesting tricks): • Section [metadata]: – experiment_id – owner • Section [dataset]: – name (the data-set’s human-readable name) – version (string, do not cast to float) • Section [model]: – architecture – param_count (leave as the literal integer string that appears in the file—no re-formatting) 1. Create a new file /home/user/experiments/experiment_summary.csv using exactly this CSV structure: • The **first** line is the fixed header (no spaces): 
experiment_id,owner,dataset_name,dataset_version,architecture,param_count
• Every following line represents one experiment, with the six fields written in that exact order, separated by **single commas** and **no extra spaces**. • Sort the experiment lines in ascending alphabetical order by experiment_id (e.g. exp001, exp002, …). • Ensure the file ends with one trailing newline character. The automated grader will parse the CSV and compare it byte-for-byte with the expected answer. Pay special attention to: • Lower- vs upper-case of keys and values (use what you read verbatim). • No leading/trailing whitespace around commas or at line ends. • Exactly one newline character at the very end of the file. • The header must be present and spelled exactly as shown. A typical shell session will involve 5–10 commands: 1) cd to the directory, 2) list sub-directories, 3) view or parse each config.ini, 4) extract the required keys (e.g. with grep, awk, python, or crudini if installed), 5) build the CSV lines and write the output file, 6) display the file (optional for human confirmation). • /home/user/experiments/experiment_summary.csv exists. • The header line is exactly as specified. • Each subsequent line contains the correct values for all experiments present on disk, ordered correctly, with the correct delimiter, and with no additional lines or spaces. • The file ends with one final newline character.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth files that already exist before the agent starts.
    -->
    <initial-filesystem>
        /home/user/experiments/
        ├── exp001/
        │   └── config.ini
        ├── exp002/
        │   └── config.ini
        └── exp003/
            └── config.ini
    </initial-filesystem>

    <file path="/home/user/experiments/exp001/config.ini"><![CDATA[
[metadata]
experiment_id = exp001
owner = alice
created = 2023-08-10

[dataset]
name = imagenet
version = 1.2

[model]
architecture = resnet50
param_count = 25557032
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/experiments/exp002/config.ini"><![CDATA[
[metadata]
experiment_id = exp002
owner = bob
created = 2023-08-11

[dataset]
name = cifar10
version = 3.0

[model]
architecture = mobilenet_v2
param_count = 3504875
]]></file>

    <file path="/home/user/experiments/exp003/config.ini"><![CDATA[
[metadata]
experiment_id = exp003
owner = carol
created = 2023-08-12

[dataset]
name = mnist
version = 1.0

[model]
architecture = lenet
param_count = 60000
]]></file>

    <!--
    File the agent must create.
    -->
    <expected-output path="/home/user/experiments/experiment_summary.csv"><![CDATA[
experiment_id,owner,dataset_name,dataset_version,architecture,param_count
exp001,alice,imagenet,1.2,resnet50,25557032
exp002,bob,cifar10,3.0,mobilenet_v2,3504875
exp003,carol,mnist,1.0,lenet,60000
]]></expected-output>
name: task_000000_ce51a6fd
